join_order.notes: Use the persisted pre_adsh_tag_version_groups index only after collapsing filtered num rows to unique anti-join keys; partition unique probe keys by the high 4 bits of the initial hash slot and process one partition at a time so each pass focuses on about 1/16 of hash.bin (~50 MB).

scan.access_pattern: postings-driven candidate enumeration with random gathers into num columns

data_structures.dim_filter: bitset(39401761) for USD membership; drive from num_ddate_postings range rowids
data_structures.fact_join: pre_built_index(pre_adsh_tag_version_groups) probed once per unique (adsh,tag,version) after partitioning probe keys by hash region
data_structures.aggregation: thread_local compact_hash_map keyed by TripleKey(adsh,tag,version) -> {count_rows,sum_value}, then compact_hash_map keyed by packed(tag,version) for final rollup

indexes.purpose: enumerate 2023 candidate rowids via postings range scan
apply USD membership before materializing candidate triples
exact anti-join existence lookup on unique (adsh,tag,version) keys

optimization_notes: Root cause: the hot path performs a cache-exceeding anti-probe into the 33,554,432-bucket pre hash table once per filtered num row, even though many filtered rows share the same (adsh, tag, version) anti-join key and therefore repeat identical existence checks. Strategy category A/C: restructure the plan to aggregate filtered num rows first by the full anti-join key, then probe pre_adsh_tag_version_groups once per unique triple, and finally roll surviving counts and sums up to (tag, version); order those unique probe keys by coarse hash-table region so each pass reuses a ~50 MB slice of hash.bin instead of thrashing across the full ~805 MB structure.
